page.title: Puerto Banus Yacht Rental in Winter: Why Off-Season Works

data.meta_description: Discover why winter is the smart season for Puerto Banus yacht rental. Enjoy calm seas, lower rates, and guaranteed berths. Book your off-peak charter today.
data.summary: Winter yacht rental in Puerto Banus offers calm seas, fewer crowds, and more availability. This guide explains the practical advantages of off-season charters and highlights our fleet options.
key_facts: Winter sea temperatures average 15-17°C, with calm conditions ideal for cruising
Puerto Banus is fully operational year-round; restaurants and bars remain open
Off-peak charters often have more flexible scheduling and last-minute availability
Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours, all inclusive
Mangusta 80 variants (24m, 12 pax) include a free jet ski on 4-hour+ bookings
Winter daylight hours in Marbella: 09:30 – 18:00 (December), longer by February
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA
No need to book months ahead; many dates are open even a week before

faq.q: Is Puerto Banus yacht rental available in winter?
faq.a: Yes, Puerto Banus operates year-round. Marinas, bars, and restaurants remain open, and our fleet is available daily. Winter is actually a great time to charter because availability is higher and the harbour is less crowded.

faq.q: What is the weather like for a winter yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Winter daytime temperatures in Marbella range from 15°C to 20°C, with sea temperatures around 15-17°C. The Mediterranean is often calmer than in summer, with fewer windy days. Sunny spells are common, and you can comfortably sit on deck with a light jacket.

faq.q: What boats are available for winter rental in Puerto Banus?
faq.a: All our boats are available year-round, including the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax), Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), and many others. Pricing for Tier A boats starts at €749 for 2 hours, all inclusive.

faq.q: Are there any discounts for winter yacht charters?
faq.a: Our published rates remain the same year-round, but off-peak demand means you can often secure your preferred dates at short notice. We do not run seasonal sales, but the value is excellent given the calm conditions and exclusive feel.

faq.q: Can we swim or use the jet ski in winter?
faq.a: Yes, you can swim, though the water is brisk. Many guests enjoy a quick dip followed by warming up with mulled wine or hot drinks. Our Mangusta 80 charters (4h+) include a free jet ski, which is still great fun in winter with a wetsuit.

faq.q: What should we wear for a winter yacht trip in Marbella?
faq.a: Bring layers: a warm jacket, scarf, and comfortable shoes. The sun can be strong midday, so sunglasses and sunscreen are still advisable. The boat’s cabin provides shelter if it gets chilly, and we provide blankets on request.

faq.q: How long are winter daylight hours in Marbella?
faq.a: In December, sunrise is around 08:30 and sunset about 18:00, giving you roughly 9.5 hours of daylight. By February, days lengthen to about 11 hours. Morning or early afternoon charters work best to maximise daylight.

faq.q: Do I need to book far in advance for a winter charter?
faq.a: No. Winter is our quietest period, so you can often book just a week ahead. We recommend contacting us via WhatsApp to check availability for your preferred date. Last-minute bookings are usually possible.

data.body_html:
Most visitors assume Puerto Banus yacht rental is a summer-only affair. The reality is different: winter offers calm seas, empty anchorages, and a more relaxed pace. With daytime temperatures hovering around 15–20°C and the Mediterranean often glassy, off-season charters deliver a different kind of luxury — one without the crowds. Whether you're looking for a private afternoon cruise or a full-day escape, winter is arguably the smartest time to book. Our fleet is available year-round from Puerto Banus, and we've outlined exactly what to expect below.

Why Winter Works for Puerto Banus Yacht Rental

The Costa del Sol enjoys a mild microclimate thanks to the Sierra Blanca mountains. Winter sea temperatures sit at 15–17°C, and the prevailing winds (Poniente) are lighter than in summer. This means fewer cancelled trips and more comfortable cruising. Puerto Banus remains fully operational — restaurants, bars, and shops stay open, and the marina is quieter, so you'll get a berth without fuss. For a puerto banus yacht rental winter experience, the off-season is a genuine advantage.

Our Fleet: What's Available in Winter

All boats in our fleet are available for winter charters. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal — both priced at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, with skipper, drinks, and snacks included. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) starts at €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter and includes a free jet ski. Other boats like the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) or Fairline Targa 12m (12m, 10 pax) are available on request — just message us via WhatsApp for pricing.

Winter Routes: Where to Cruise

Popular winter routes include heading west towards Estepona (about 12 NM from Puerto Banus) or east to Marbella Marina (5 NM). The coast is dotted with quiet coves like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, where you can anchor for lunch or a quick swim. With fewer boats around, you'll often have these spots to yourself. For a longer trip, the Golden Mile coastline offers stunning views of La Concha mountain, and the water is calm enough for a relaxed cruise even in January.

What's Included in Every Winter Charter

Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You won't face hidden costs. In winter, we can also provide hot drinks like coffee or tea on request. The boat's cabin is heated, so you can warm up between deck sessions. There's no surcharge for winter charters — the same all-inclusive rates apply year-round.

Practical Tips for a Winter Yacht Trip

  • Dress in layers: A warm jacket, scarf, and comfortable shoes are essential. The sun can be strong, so bring sunglasses and sunscreen.
  • Book morning or early afternoon: December daylight runs from 08:30 to 18:00; February extends to about 11 hours. Aim for a 10:00 or 12:00 start to maximise sunshine.
  • Check the forecast: While generally calm, winter can bring occasional rain. We monitor conditions and will advise if rescheduling is wise — no penalties for weather-related changes.
  • Bring a camera: Winter light is beautiful, and the mountains behind Marbella are often snow-capped, creating dramatic photo opportunities.

Why Choose Us for Your Winter Charter

We operate our own fleet from Puerto Banus — we're not an agency. This means you deal directly with us for booking, payment, and any questions. Our skippers know the local waters intimately and can tailor the route to your preferences. Winter charters are more relaxed, and we often have same-week availability. To check dates or ask about a specific boat, visit our homepage or message us on WhatsApp. For more seasonal advice, read our guide to Puerto Banus yacht rental in winter.

Frequently asked questions

Is Puerto Banus yacht rental available in winter?

Yes, Puerto Banus operates year-round. Marinas, bars, and restaurants remain open, and our fleet is available daily. Winter is actually a great time to charter because availability is higher and the harbour is less crowded.

What is the weather like for a winter yacht charter in Marbella?

Winter daytime temperatures in Marbella range from 15°C to 20°C, with sea temperatures around 15-17°C. The Mediterranean is often calmer than in summer, with fewer windy days. Sunny spells are common, and you can comfortably sit on deck with a light jacket.

What boats are available for winter rental in Puerto Banus?

All our boats are available year-round, including the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax), Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), and many others. Pricing for Tier A boats starts at €749 for 2 hours, all inclusive.

Are there any discounts for winter yacht charters?

Our published rates remain the same year-round, but off-peak demand means you can often secure your preferred dates at short notice. We do not run seasonal sales, but the value is excellent given the calm conditions and exclusive feel.

Can we swim or use the jet ski in winter?

Yes, you can swim, though the water is brisk. Many guests enjoy a quick dip followed by warming up with mulled wine or hot drinks. Our Mangusta 80 charters (4h+) include a free jet ski, which is still great fun in winter with a wetsuit.

What should we wear for a winter yacht trip in Marbella?

Bring layers: a warm jacket, scarf, and comfortable shoes. The sun can be strong midday, so sunglasses and sunscreen are still advisable. The boat’s cabin provides shelter if it gets chilly, and we provide blankets on request.

How long are winter daylight hours in Marbella?

In December, sunrise is around 08:30 and sunset about 18:00, giving you roughly 9.5 hours of daylight. By February, days lengthen to about 11 hours. Morning or early afternoon charters work best to maximise daylight.

Do I need to book far in advance for a winter charter?

No. Winter is our quietest period, so you can often book just a week ahead. We recommend contacting us via WhatsApp to check availability for your preferred date. Last-minute bookings are usually possible.
